architecture_fit.note: Single attention head, one additive relative-position bias, no MLP — minimal delta from base_model that nails prev-token mass at 0.999.

baseline_comparison.note: Content-blind zero-logit uniform strawman measured under identical conditions lands at 0.0594 baseline, ~17x below the head.

faithfulness.note: Bias-center sweep c in {0,1,2,3} shows the attention peak walks self->prev->two-back — a clean synthetic activation-patch analogue pinning offset 1 as the cause.

operating_range.note: Noise sweep is perfectly flat (robustness 1.0) but only because content is ignored by construction; seq_len/scale axis not stressed, though honestly argued.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Mechanism is fully hand-set (alpha, center=1) with no learning — exactly the hand-built circuit the bonus rewards.

visual_judgement.note: Heatmap (bright sub-diagonal), robustness line plot vs baseline/distractors, and causal ablation bars together make the claim legible at a glance.

visualisation_rationale.note: README ties each panel to a specific goal question (hits i-1?, holds under noise?, is it causal?) with the right axes and grain.

notes: An exemplary hand-built previous-token head: a content-blind relative-position bias peaked at offset 1 that hits 0.999 prev-token mass with robustness 1.0, plus a uniform strawman and a bias-center ablation that causally locates the mechanism. The only soft spot is operating range — robustness is flat by construction (content ignored) rather than demonstrated under genuine mechanism stress, and seq_len is fixed. Strong, legible, well-justified throughout; lands at 'good'.
